metadata.name: djangoldp-account
metadata.summary: djangoldp package for account and chat data models

documentation: gets a hold of an access token & id token

documentation: the test where all with the request is as it should be

functions.name: djangoldp_account.views.check_user
documentation: Returns user if they are authenticated with this server, else 404

functions.name: djangoldp_account.views._set_default_redirect_uri
documentation: auxiliary function to set a user's default_redirect_uri

classes.name: djangoldp_account.views.RedirectView
documentation:
View for managing where to redirect the user after a successful login
In the event that 'next' is not set (they may be coming from one of multiple front-end apps)

To use this functionality set LOGIN_REDIRECT_URL to a url which uses this View. Then if the user
has not set 'next' parameter in the login request, Django will redirect them here

classes.name: djangoldp_account.views.LDPAccountLoginView

methods.name: djangoldp_account.views.LDPAccountLoginView.post
documentation: Save login url as preferred redirect
Extension of django.contrib.auth.views.LoginView for managing user's default_redirect_uri

classes.name: djangoldp_account.views.LDPAccountRegistrationView

methods.name: djangoldp_account.views.LDPAccountRegistrationView.get_success_url
documentation: Return the URL to redirect to after successful redirection.

methods.name: djangoldp_account.views.LDPAccountRegistrationView.get_redirect_url
documentation: Return the user-originating redirect URL if it's safe.
Extension of django-registration's RegistrationView for managing user's default_redirect_uri

classes.name: djangoldp_account.views.RPLoginView
documentation:
RP authentication workflow
See https://github.com/solid/webid-oidc-spec/blob/master/example-workflow.md
Wa're using oid module : https://pyoidc.readthedocs.io/en/latest/examples/rp.html

classes.name: djangoldp_account.views.RPLoginCallBackView
documentation: After a login request is made (on RPLoginEndpoint.op_login_url) the OP will redirect the user to this view

methods.name: djangoldp_account.auth.solid.Solid.check_id_token_exp
documentation: raises LDPLoginError if parameterised expiry time has passed

methods.name: djangoldp_account.auth.solid.Solid.confirm_webid
documentation:
validates that the Identity Provider (the value in the issuer claim) is authorized by the holder of the WebID
See <https://github.com/solid/webid-oidc-spec/blob/master/README.md#webid-provider-confirmation>
\:param webid: the webfinger id provided
\:param iss: the issuer, identity provider
\:return: None
\:raises: LDPLoginError if unable to confirm the webid

methods.name: djangoldp_account.auth.solid.Solid.get_or_create_user
documentation:
retrieves an existing local copy of the user or creates one. Useful in federation- I have logged in with
a user account from another provider to access this server
\:return: User object (of type configured by server settings)

functions.name: djangoldp_account.forms._get_user_form_fields
documentation: Gets the fields required for the form from settings, or returns default

methods.name: djangoldp_account.endpoints.webfinger.AccountWebFinger.response
documentation: responds to web finger request with user and OIDC issuer information

methods.name: djangoldp_account.models.LDPUser.save
documentation:
This is a workaround for the LDPSerializer which enforce the user to post an username when creating a new one
By setting the username to `generate-an-username` we ensure that the user will have an username in conformity
with slugify & the federation requirement

classes.name: djangoldp_account.endpoints.rp_login.RPLoginEndpoint

methods.name: djangoldp_account.endpoints.rp_login.RPLoginEndpoint.validate_params
documentation:
A provider must be set.
It can be a url (auth provider)
or email or webId (In those case, provider discovery is necessary)

See: 2.1 https://github.com/solid/webid-oidc-spec/blob/master/example-workflow.md

methods.name: djangoldp_account.endpoints.rp_login.RPLoginEndpoint.op_login_url
documentation:
Resolves the URL for making a login request with the OIDC Provider of a parameterised subject
Performs issuer discovery if required

methods.name: djangoldp_account.endpoints.rp_login.RPLoginEndpoint.check_on_known_providers
documentation:
attempts to find a provider/OP for parameterised subject. Uses pyoidc's discover method with the resource
on each stored OPClient object

RP login endpoint
This is *not* the OP login endpoint (OP part is managed by django_oidc)

methods.name: djangoldp_account.endpoints.rp_login.RPLoginCallBackEndpoint.initial_url
documentation: function executed after receiving the callback from the OIDC Provider

methods.name: djangoldp_account.endpoints.rp_login.RPLoginCallBackEndpoint.extract_web_id
documentation:
See <https://github.com/solid/webid-oidc-spec/blob/master/README.md#method-1---custom-webid-claim>
\:param userinfo: UserInfo object returned by the OIDC Provider in do_user_info_request
\:return:
